name: query
context: Query Prometheus metrics, targets, rules, alerts, and TSDB state through the official HTTP API.
info: This skill wraps the Prometheus HTTP API endpoints an autonomous agent actually needs in practice: instant and range queries for PromQL expressions, series inspection for label troubleshooting, target health for scrape debugging, rules and alerts for operational state, and status endpoints for loaded config and TSDB health. It is the fastest path from a monitoring question to evidence.
body:
# Query Prometheus

Prometheus exposes a JSON HTTP API under `/api/v1`. The most important endpoints are `query` for instant evaluation, `query_range` for windowed data, `series` for label-set inspection, and status endpoints for runtime introspection.

```bash
curl -g --data-urlencode 'query=up{job="node"}' "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/query"
curl -g "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/query_range?query=rate(http_requests_total[5m])&start=2026-04-12T00:00:00Z&end=2026-04-12T01:00:00Z&step=30s"
curl "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/targets"
curl "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/status/tsdb"
```

The provided script covers the same endpoints with auth and TLS environment variables:

```bash
python scripts/query-api.py instant --query 'up{job="node"}'
python scripts/query-api.py range --query 'rate(http_requests_total[5m])' --start 2026-04-12T00:00:00Z --end 2026-04-12T01:00:00Z --step 30s
python scripts/query-api.py targets
python scripts/query-api.py status-config
```

Query results return a `resultType` such as `vector` or `matrix`. Empty results are still valid success responses. When the issue is about missing metrics rather than bad PromQL, check `targets` before rewriting the query.

sections.title: Instant vs range queries
sections.body: Use `instant` for point-in-time answers such as `up`, saturation right now, or a single `sum by (...)` value. Use `range` when you need behavior across a time window, chart data, or derivatives like `rate` over a window. The official API supports `query`, `time`, `timeout`, and `limit` for instant queries, and adds `start`, `end`, and `step` for range queries.

sections.title: Targets, rules, alerts, and status
sections.body: Operational debugging often starts with non-PromQL endpoints: `/api/v1/targets` shows whether the scrape is healthy, `/api/v1/rules` shows loaded rule groups, `/api/v1/alerts` shows active alert state, `/api/v1/status/config` shows what config the server loaded, and `/api/v1/status/tsdb` exposes cardinality and head stats. Agents should inspect these before changing config blind.

sections.title: Result shapes
sections.body: Prometheus JSON responses use `data.resultType` to describe what came back: `vector` for instant vectors, `matrix` for range vectors, `scalar` for a single numeric value, and `string` for a string response. Empty `data.result` arrays are not errors. When a query is valid but too broad, the server may return warnings or perform badly because the selector expands to too many series.

scripts.name: Query Prometheus HTTP API
scripts.description: Run instant and range queries plus status and target inspection against a Prometheus server

PROMETHEUS_BEARER_TOKEN.description: Bearer token if a proxy or auth layer requires it

PROMETHEUS_BASIC_AUTH_USERNAME.description: Basic auth username for protected endpoints

PROMETHEUS_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD.description: Basic auth password for protected endpoints

PROMETHEUS_CA_BUNDLE.description: Custom CA bundle for HTTPS connections

PROMETHEUS_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for debugging only

query.description: PromQL expression for instant or range actions

matchers.description: Series selectors for the series action

step.description: Range query resolution step, for example 30s

depends.reason: Instant and range actions rely on valid PromQL expressions
Protected deployments require auth or TLS setup before the API can be queried

author: dojo-community
